Private — Career Guidance Test
Jess
Project Manager  ·  Tech  ·  Mid-career (5–15 years)
She had already made her decision to leave, offer in hand. Then her company offered to sponsor her for an MBA if she stayed, right as she was on her way out the door. Now she has a matter of days to decide whether to walk away from something she has already accepted, or reconsider based on an offer that showed up almost too late to matter.
Why we thought of you She asked to hear from women further along in their career who have been in a moment like this, where a company suddenly steps up right as you are on your way out the door. We chose you because your experience is directly relevant to what she is weighing.
Please take a moment to read her question carefully. The more context you have, the more useful your response will be to her.
Read her full question ⌄
Her situation
I decided to leave my current role. The stagnation had been building for a while and I have an offer on the table, slightly higher salary, a role that feels like real progress. I was ready.
The challenge
Then leadership offered to sponsor me for an MBA if I stay. I wasn't asking for it and the timing feels reactive. But I also don't want to walk away from something real just because I'm impatient, or because the offer came too late to matter.
What she is looking for
I want to hear from women who have been in a moment like this, where the company suddenly showed up right as they were leaving. How did you read it? How did you decide? How did it pan out? How would you choose next time? What you wish you had thought of back then?
Something else worth knowing
I've verbally accepted the other offer but haven't signed yet. I have a week, at most 10 days.
What would be most useful to her
Their personal experience A candid read
Please open and read her full question before continuing.
About this experiment ⌄
This is a private experiment. Lucy is running this personally and will be the only other person to see what you share before it reaches Jess. Depending on how much you want to say, this whole experience should take no more than a few minutes. We have tried to make it as easy as possible.
Technical issue? Email lucydesigning@gmail.com

Step 3 of 5
Share what you know.
Voice is encouraged. It tends to feel more personal and is easier to give honestly. Written is equally welcome. Aim for around 2 to 4 minutes if speaking, or 200 to 300 words if writing.
Record it
Voice note, encouraged

In her words
"I don't want to walk away from something real just because I'm impatient, or because the offer came too late to matter."
She is looking for: your personal experience and a candid read on her situation. Start wherever feels natural. A moment from your own path, what you wish someone had told you, or a straight read on how you see what she's weighing.

We are grateful you said yes. When you find yourself in a place of needing help, we hope you come back.
